posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Dumont vs. Santos
Norma Dumont, DEC
UFC might have to rig another Dumont win on the scorecards again here but it is what it is that juicy booty is getting a title shot after this whether we like it or not.

Vieira vs. McConico
Rodolfo Vieira, DEC
Jesus Christ dude if this remains a striking match then were just gonna watch these two bums stare at eachother for 15 minutes. Gut feeling tells me Viera wins just because McConnico should be 0-3 in the UFC right now.

Filho vs. Rocha
Jafel Filho, SUB, R2
I remember Clayton Carpenter making this guy look horrible. Easy sub for Filho here.

Dumas vs. McVey
Jackson McVey, SUB, R1
OMG the amount of matchups on here between literal jobbers is crazy. I think McVey uses his size and BJJ to snatch up a submission here early. Dumas has ridiculously bad choke defense and struggled with previous opponents of a similar skill set.

Grant vs. Luna Martinetti
Adrian Luna Martinetti, DEC
Wow an actual banger matchup on this card looks so out of place. My heart wants to pick Davey here but I actually think this new guy is going to be pretty good. Showed a lot of heart and the ability to put huge striking numbers up over the course of 3 rounds on his DWCS win. Davey being 40 and coming off a bad KO loss makes me worry a bit too much in this matchup against a young and hungry opponent entering his prime.

posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Brady vs. Buckley
Sean Brady, DEC
Man... This is one of those fights that I cant feel too confident picking either side based on the matchup and how they looked in their last performance. We're gonna find out real early if Brady's glass jaw or Buckley's shoddy defense is bigger Achilles heel. I could definitely see a world where Buckley steps on the gas right away, pressures him, puts his back against the cage and finds the TKO but I think the smarter pick here is Brady by decision. We've seen him raggdoll and be dominant against stronger grapplers than Buckley and I just dont trust Buckley's fight IQ at times. If Brady gets chinned horrifically again though I could see a potential Gilbert Burns or Luque level of fall off in the near future.

posts.author: B7L7
posts.content:
[Predictions]

If this was a 3 round fight it would really favor Aljo, but I think at this point in his career he can't sustain high pace across 5 rounds, and he seems less durable than ever. He got tired in a 3 round fight against Movsar which is normal since it was full of super high paced chain-wrestling sequences, but to me it's super clear why he fought with low pace and somewhat low output in a dominant win against Ortega: he simply doesn't have 5 round cardio.  I don't think Zalal gets tired easily and is a good striker at range. Good luck to Aljo trying to catch him across the octagon for 5 rounds without completely gassing out. If he can get the takedonws in early and win rounds 1-3 by establishing significant ground control time, then Zalal should be tired aswell, but I don't think it will happen.

* Edited at 03.12.2026,  9:37 AM ET *

posts.author: battousai
posts.content:
@Bilinho
You're over analyzing the fact that Aljo had to fly to the other side of the planet for a fight where he didn't even complete his weight cut. I don't think Aljo was probably super locked in for that one. There was a point where he probably thought he flew halfway around the world for nothing and doubted whether the fight would even happen.

Aljo took a round off Evloev and I don't remember him looking anymore tired than Evloev afterwards. That fight was just super competitive. No offense, but it feels like you're trying to manufacture a narrative here that's not supported by much evidence. I think there's honestly a better argument to be made we know 0 about Zalal's sustained cardio in a long, drawn out 5 round fight. Zalal has only seen a 3rd round once in the last +3 years and is 4-5 in his career in fights that go the distance.

I could easily see Aljo taking 3 rounds from him with how good of a grappler he is. That is what Zalal has been coasting off of his most recent run but he's not just gonna coast against Aljo. Zalal has also just faced 0 adversity in a really long time and I could see him struggling here against someone who's got multiple main event wins banked at this level. Just my personal opinion, but it's hard not to lean on Aljo's elite experience here.

I lean savvy vet performance by Aljo here, 3-2 or 4-1 on the scorecards.

posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Sterling vs. Zalal
Aljamain Sterling, DEC
Oh god this going to be hard to watch for 5 rounds but I think Aljo is the much superior fighter here in pretty much every aspect. Aljo is insanely strong and his grappling dominance hasnt skipped a beat since moving up to 145lbs. I clown on him a lot for being boring but honestly this guy is one of the most dominant fighters of this era and one of the best MMA grapplers of all time. Zalal is good and very well rounded no doubt but he's got pillow hands which plays a part in this because the guys who have beaten Aljo are typically knocking him out or hurting him badly on the feet. I think Aljo mop up the octagon with Zala via wrestling dominance here but I tell you what; if Zalal wins this one Im predicting right now that he's a future champ. The Belal comparisons were a funny joke at first but the parallels between the two are becoming scary.

Jackson vs. Barcelos
Montel Jackson, TKO, R2
IDK what on earth Jackson was doing last fight but he's such a tough matchup for Raoni that I kind of lean towards him. Barcelos is old and kind of chinny and he's won his most recent fights using his grappling but idk if he's gonna have that same success against Jackson in this one. If Jackson keeps this one on the feet he should knock out Barcelos.

Dumont vs. Edwards
Norma Dumont, DEC
Honestly.... This is a #1 contender fight right now at 135lbs. Both women are on long win streaks at the moment and the division is lacking depth big time. I think Norma gets it done here because based off her resume she's been in there with fighters more talented than Edwards has plus she has more "pretty privilege" which I know is a thing now in the UFC considering Dern is champ.

posts.author: battousai
posts.content:
Co-main is a great example of how quickly consistently finishing fights will get you noticed. Dumont is arguably the #1 contender just by virtue of her win streak. However, Allen Perez is now tied with her and at least has 1 finish to her credit. Shes also already beaten Edwards.

Yet 4 finishes in a row has granted Edwards the fight Perez herself has been calling for. Just goes to show you one loss doesn't mean anything and that a fighters path can change very quickly.
